skill.name: moa-explainer
skill.description: Generate 3D animation scripts and lay explanations for drug mechanisms.
skill.summary: Generate structured animation storyboards and plain-language explanations for any drug mechanism of action.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 43 potential issues across 4 files (640 lines) with a risk score of 100/100, driven entirely by pattern overmatch. All 10 high-severity findings are false positives: 'weak cryptographic algorithm' matched unrelated keywords in documentation, 'path traversal' matched the skill's own security checklist items containing '../', and 'shell backtick execution' matched markdown inline code formatting (28 locations). The only legitimate finding is a file write operation at scripts/main.py:117 that accepts a user-controlled output path, which is expected behavior for this text-generation tool and operates within a sandboxed environment. No malicious intent, credential theft, data exfiltration, or prompt injection was detected.

low_findings.title: User-Controlled File Write Path
low_findings.description: The script at scripts/main.py:117 writes generated output to a file path specified by the user via the --output argument. This is the intended behavior of the skill and operates within a sandboxed execution environment. No path sanitization is applied, but in the Claude Code sandbox this cannot escape the workspace boundary.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backtick Formatting Flagged as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 28 inline code backticks in SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. Every instance is markdown formatting (e.g., `scripts/main.py`, `Python`), not command substitution. These are false positives caused by pattern overmatch on backtick characters.

dangerous_patterns.title: Security Checklist Items Flagged as Path Traversal
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md lines 117 and 120 as path traversal because they contain the string '../'. These lines are part of the skill's own security checklist: 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'. They document security protections, not exploits.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation URL Flagged as Hardcoded Network Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 7 as a hardcoded URL. This is a GitHub source repository link in the document header, which is standard documentation practice and not a security concern.

content.user_title: Generate Drug Mechanism Animation Scripts and Explanations
content.value_statement: Explaining how drugs work at the molecular level takes time and expertise. This skill generates structured 3D animation storyboards and plain-language explanations for any drug mechanism, making scientific communication faster and more consistent.
actual_capabilities: Generate structured 3D animation storyboards with timed scenes for drug mechanisms of action
Produce plain-language explanations of drug mechanisms tailored to expert, lay, or patient audiences
Create voiceover scripts synchronized with animation scene descriptions and timing
Accept configurable parameters including drug name, molecular target, mechanism, and audience level
Output reusable text artifacts suitable for medical education, sales training, and patient materials
Provide a reproducible command-line workflow via scripts/main.py with documented fallback paths
limitations: Does not produce actual 3D renderings, video files, or graphical animation assets
Requires accurate drug mechanism information as input to generate meaningful output
Output quality depends on the specificity and completeness of the mechanism description provided
Generated content should be reviewed by a subject matter expert before use in regulated materials

use_cases.title: Medical Education Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate educational materials explaining drug mechanisms to medical students and healthcare professionals with scientifically accurate animation storyboards.

use_cases.title: Pharmaceutical Sales Training
use_cases.description: Create training scripts and visual concept guides for sales representatives learning about new drug products and their mechanisms of action.

use_cases.title: Patient Education Materials
use_cases.description: Produce simple, accessible explanations of how medications work using everyday analogies suitable for patient information leaflets and websites.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Drug Mechanism Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 3D animation script for the drug semaglutide. Target: GLP-1 receptor. Mechanism: semaglutide binds to and activates the GLP-1 receptor, enhancing glucose-dependent insulin secretion and slowing gastric emptying. Audience: expert.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a fast animation storyboard for a known drug with a well-understood mechanism.

prompt_templates.title: Lay Explanation for Patients
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how metformin works in plain language suitable for a patient with type 2 diabetes. Drug: metformin. Mechanism: metformin reduces glucose production in the liver and improves insulin sensitivity in peripheral tissues. Audience: patient.
prompt_templates.scenario: You are creating patient education materials and need a simple, clear explanation.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Drug Comparison Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create animation scripts comparing three common blood pressure medications. Drug 1: lisinopril (ACE inhibitor). Drug 2: losartan (ARB). Drug 3: amlodipine (calcium channel blocker). Show how each drug targets a different part of the same pathway. Audience: expert.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a comparative educational resource showing how different drug classes address the same condition.

prompt_templates.title: Full Educational Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete educational package for the PD-1 inhibitor pembrolizumab. Include: a 6-scene 3D animation storyboard with timing, a voiceover script for each scene, a simplified patient summary, and a list of key visual concepts for the animation team. Target: PD-1 receptor. Mechanism: pembrolizumab blocks PD-1, preventing cancer cells from deactivating T-cells, which restores anti-tumor immune response. Disease: melanoma. Audience: mixed expert and patient.
prompt_templates.scenario: You are preparing a comprehensive communication package for an investor presentation or product launch.

output_examples.input: Generate an animation script for atorvastatin. Target: HMG-CoA reductase. Mechanism: atorvastatin competitively inhibits HMG-CoA reductase, blocking the conversion of HMG-CoA to mevalonate and reducing cholesterol synthesis in the liver. Audience: expert.
output: SCENE 1: INTRODUCTION — Show normal hepatocyte environment with HMG-CoA reductase enzyme in its active conformation. Duration: 5 seconds.
SCENE 2: DISEASE STATE — Depict elevated cholesterol production with excessive HMG-CoA reductase activity. Visual: enzyme molecules highlighted with overactive glow. Duration: 5 seconds.
SCENE 3: DRUG ENTRY — Atorvastatin molecule enters the hepatocyte, structurally similar to HMG-CoA. Visual: drug highlighted with tracking glow effect. Duration: 3 seconds.

SCENE 4: MECHANISM OF ACTION — Atorvastatin competitively binds to HMG-CoA reductase active site, blocking substrate access. Visual: conformational change prevents HMG-CoA from binding. Duration: 10 seconds.
SCENE 5: THERAPEUTIC EFFECT — Downstream cholesterol synthesis pathway slows. LDL receptor expression increases. Plasma LDL levels decrease. Duration: 5 seconds.
SCENE 6: SUMMARY — Recap competitive inhibition mechanism and net effect on cholesterol homeostasis. Duration: 3 seconds.

output_examples.input: Explain how insulin works for a patient newly diagnosed with diabetes. Drug: insulin. Mechanism: insulin binds to insulin receptors on cell surfaces, triggering GLUT4 translocation to the membrane and enabling glucose uptake from the blood into cells. Audience: patient.
output_examples.output:
HOW INSULIN WORKS (Simplified)

Imagine your body's cells are like houses with locked doors. Glucose, the sugar your body uses for energy, is outside on the street and needs to get inside.

THE PROBLEM:
Without enough insulin, the doors to your cells stay locked. Sugar builds up in your blood instead of entering your cells where it belongs.

THE SOLUTION:
Insulin acts like a key that unlocks these doors. When you take insulin, it travels through your blood and fits into special locks, called receptors, on the surface of your cells.

WHAT HAPPENS:
Once insulin turns the key, your cells open special glucose doorways that let sugar move from your blood into the cell. Think of it like opening grocery store doors so deliveries can come inside.

THE RESULT:
Your cells get the energy they need to work properly, and the sugar level in your blood returns to a healthy range.

best_practices: Provide the drug name, molecular target, and mechanism of action as separate, clearly labeled inputs for the most accurate output.
Specify the target audience before generating content so the language and complexity level are appropriate.
Review all generated scripts and explanations with a subject matter expert before using them in production materials or regulated communications.
anti_patterns: Using generated animation scripts or explanations as medical advice without independent expert review and verification.
Providing vague or incomplete mechanism descriptions that result in generic, low-value output with insufficient scientific detail.
Expecting the skill to produce actual 3D animation files, rendered videos, or graphical assets rather than text scripts and storyboards.

faq.question: What does this skill actually generate?
faq.answer: This skill generates text-based 3D animation storyboards with timed scenes, voiceover scripts, and plain-language explanations of drug mechanisms. It does not render actual animation files or video.

faq.question: What inputs do I need to provide?
faq.answer: You need to provide the drug name, the molecular target, the mechanism of action description, and the target audience level such as expert, lay, or patient.

faq.question: Can this skill create actual 3D animation files?
faq.answer: No. This skill produces text scripts and storyboards that an animator or visualization team would use to create the actual 3D animation. It does not render graphics or video.

faq.question: What audience levels are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports expert audiences that need scientific detail, lay audiences that need simplified explanations, and patient audiences that need accessible language with helpful analogies.

faq.question: Is medical review required for the output?
faq.answer: Yes. Generated content should be reviewed by a qualified medical professional before use in patient-facing materials, regulatory submissions, or published educational content.

faq.question: What types of drugs can this skill explain?
faq.answer: The skill can explain any drug mechanism including small molecules, biologics, monoclonal antibodies, gene therapies, and other therapeutic modalities, as long as you provide an accurate mechanism description.
